channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19114] conf-sanity test_54a: mds1: import is not in FULL state after 50
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/bd9defcb-b8e8-4224-92a5-aa90c2bd13ed

test_54a failed with the following error:

mds1: import is not in FULL state after 50

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master-next/891 - 4.18.0-553.51.1.el8_10.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master-next/891 - 4.18.0-553.51.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

Started on master-next on 2025-06-08 and then moved to master on 2015-06-12.

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
conf-sanity test_54a - mds1: import is not in FULL state after 50
item.summary: conf-sanity test_54a: mds1: import is not in FULL state after 50

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: Hmm, this subtest has not failed once in the 83 sessions on master+ldiskfs between 2025-06-15..2025-06-19, so it is possible that there was another patch landed on 2025-06-17 that fixed it? I don't see any patches with a Fixes: label that references one of the above patches, but it may be prudent to wait and see if this reproduces again, before investing any more time into it.

Based on the interop test versions where this subtest fails, it appears that this is a server-side bug:

Patches landed to master on 2025-06-12 that affect the server code:

fb8423bfd9 LU-19091 ptlrpc: protect internal access to obd->obd_svc_stats
3f2bfd1b0e LU-19076 ptlrpc: resend can hit original req
919c5d25fc LU-19015 llog: logic for skipping a zeroed record
719f6763fe LU-18756 sec: add resource id check to oss and mds
36431c4948 LU-18756 sec: add generic nodemap resource id check
8f819877be LU-18986 mgs: server part of new registration protocol
8411874497 LU-18986 mgs: new target registration protocol
14b519b52d LU-17410 sec: per-nodemap capabilities mask
07f6a59ad7 LU-18556 hsm: optimize llog record modification
fff7cd33bb LU-19050 utils: Support long nid lists when getting fs info

Between 2025-06-12 and 2025-06-14 this test failed 5/46 test runs on ldiskfs+master servers, so about 1/10 runs. First it makes sense to push a patch on the tip of master with the following test parameters to see if it can be reproduced when running only this subtest:

Test-Parameters: testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=54a,ONLY_REPEAT=20
Test-Parameters: testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=54a,ONLY_REPEAT=20
Test-Parameters: testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=54a,ONLY_REPEAT=20

If that works, great, otherwise the range of subtests to be run would need to increase until the test fails, using ONLY=30-55 or ONLY=20-55 or similar.

Next would be to push test patches on various of the above commits to see where the problem is introduced.
